current_enterprise_trust_sources.requirement: Use documented governance, measurement, evaluation, monitoring, and risk-management evidence before AI trust claims.

current_enterprise_trust_sources.source: NIST AI RMF Trustworthy AI in Critical Infrastructure Profile concept note
current_enterprise_trust_sources.requirement: For critical-infrastructure-facing AI, emphasize TEVV, adversarial robustness, explainability, graceful degradation, supply-chain visibility, and human oversight.
Provide a secure-development vocabulary and repeatable SDLC evidence that purchasers can use during acquisition.
Answer product-security procurement questions and show secure-by-design ownership, transparency, and accountability.

current_enterprise_trust_sources.source: OWASP GenAI Security Project / LLM Top 10
current_enterprise_trust_sources.requirement: Cover prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, insecure output handling, excessive agency, overreliance, model theft, and related GenAI security risks.
Treat AI governance as a management-system readiness path; do not claim certification or audited AIMS without external proof.

acceptance_gates.evidence: 6 current enterprise AI/security source anchors attached.
acceptance_gates.next_action: Refresh source anchors when NIST, CISA, OWASP, or ISO guidance changes.
Use the trust pack as the internal procurement-questionnaire map only.
Owner fills and approves required procurement documents before any enterprise-ready claim.
Clear release holds with owner-approved docs, hosted proof, RLS proof, AI action policy approval, and claim-language review.
Owner approves classifications and migration, then run local and linked RLS proof rows.
Run owner-approved hosted LLM red-team smoke before external security claims.

acceptance_gates.evidence: action_surfaces=10; policy_surfaces=10; approval_required=9; direct_llm_to_irreversible_actions=0; owner_approved_policies=0; hosted_verified_tests=0.
acceptance_gates.next_action: Owner approves high-impact action policy and runs hosted no-autonomous-action boundary tests.
Clear hosted access preflight and hosted operational proof validation.
Keep enterprise-ready, hosted-live, certified, and security-proof language blocked until evidence validators pass.
Owner can review, approve, and attach procurement documents and then run hosted/RLS evidence gates.

release_holds.evidence_needed: Eight owner-approved procurement documents covering privacy/data processing, data inventory, retention, DPA/subprocessors, support/SLA, incident response, secure SDLC, and external-share approval.
Owner-approved external-share status for every artifact that may be shown to buyers.
Executed local and linked RLS proof register rows with migration marker and redacted logs.
Hosted runtime LLM red-team rows for prompt injection, disclosure, output handling, excessive agency, overreliance, and unbounded consumption.
Owner-approved high-impact action policy plus hosted no-autonomous-action boundary tests.
Hosted access preflight success and validated hosted operational proof with deploy binding and redacted logs/screenshots.
Owner-approved external wording after procurement, hosted, RLS, and AI runtime gates pass.

owner_action_order: Review the procurement evidence register and attach or approve the eight required documents.
Mark which artifacts are externally shareable and verify they contain no secrets or unsupported claims.
Approve RLS table classifications and policy draft, then run local and linked RLS proof rows.
Approve the high-impact AI action policy and run hosted no-autonomous-action boundary tests.
Clear hosted access preflight and run hosted LLM/security smoke with redacted evidence.
Rerun enterprise procurement evidence validation, this execution-readiness gate, claim consistency, and commercial confidence before changing enterprise/security language.

proves: The repo has an internal trust pack, procurement questionnaire, procurement register template, RLS proof plan, local LLM red-team proof, and AI action policy draft ready for owner review.
Enterprise/security proof remains claim-blocked until owner documents, hosted proof, RLS proof, and AI runtime proof are executed and validated.
does_not_prove: Enterprise readiness, SOC 2/ISO certification, tenant-isolation proof, hosted runtime proof, buyer-share approval, or public-sector procurement acceptance.
That privacy/DPA/SLA/incident-response documents exist or are owner-approved.
